title: O Bonnie Was Yon Rosy Brier.
content:
I.

        O Bonnie was yon rosy brier,
            That blooms sae far frae haunt o' man,
        And bonnie she, and ah, how dear!
            It shaded frae the e'enin sun.

II.

        Yon rosebuds in the morning dew
            How pure, amang the leaves sae green:
        But purer was the lover's vow
            They witness'd in their shade yestreen.

III.

        All in its rude and prickly bower,
            That crimson rose, how sweet and fair!
        But love is far a sweeter flower
            Amid life's thorny path o' care.

IV.

        The pathless wild, and wimpling burn,
            Wi' Chloris in my arms, be mine;
        And I the world, nor wish, nor scorn,
            Its joys and griefs alike resign.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "O Bonnie Was Yon Rosy Brier."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
